entry.title: From Snail Mail to Speedy Billing
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced digital transformation landscape, it is imperative for enterprises to adopt agile and robust solutions that enhance their operational efficiency. SAP has been leading customers' digital transformations across industries for over 50 years. While our solutions cover most customer needs, we also rely on a strong partner ecosystem to complete end-to-end processes. How does SAP manage this? What if a centuries-old postal service could show us the way?

The Transformation Journey of CTT Correios de Portugal

Let’s dive into the story of CTT Correios de Portugal and see how they went from old-school mail delivery to cutting-edge billing innovation. SAP Convergent Mediation by Digital Route, an extension of SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management (BRIM), is transforming the way businesses handle their billing.

Originally focused on mail delivery, the rise of digital technologies pushed CTT Correios to evolve into the parcel distribution sector. To accomplish this, they needed a powerful billing system to support their new business model.

How Did SAP Convergent Mediation Help?

From its inception in 2016 and successful go-live in April 2019, the implementation of SAP BRIM and SAP Convergent Mediation has enabled CTT Correios to revolutionize their billing processes, particularly within their parcel distribution sector. Below are key takeaways from their journey:

  • Enhancing Data Quality: SAP Convergent Mediation by Digital Route acts as a filter that processes and sorts approximately 200,000 events daily, subsequently integrating around 70,000 events into SAP for invoicing. This rigorous filtering ensures only reliable data proceeds further into the system.
  • Handling High Volumes and Complex Pricing: For CTT, the mediation tool  processes high transaction volumes, enriched data and corrected errors, thereby preventing data quality issues that plagued their previous system.
  • Real-time Integration: One of the significant benefits that CTT experienced was the real-time integration of information, which allowed them to move from hourly updates to immediate information flow. This made their operations speedy not only for internal customers but also enhanced the overall billing process efficiency.
  • Revenue Assurance: Revenue leakage was a big pain point for CTT. Just like a drip in a faucet can waste gallons of water over time, small issues in billing data can lead to significant revenue losses. SAP Convergent Mediation ensures every bit of data is accurate, preventing revenue leakage and ensuring every dollar is accounted for.
  • Scalability and Future-readiness: As they plan to move to the cloud, CTT needed a system that could enable growth. The scalability feature of SAP Convergent Mediation became crucial to support future expansion and integration with new business units, making it an integral part of CTT’s long-term strategy.

A Step Forward in Digital Transformation

Deploying SAP Convergent Mediation as an extension of BRIM has evidently yielded transformative results for CTT Correios de Portugal. Their ability to quickly adapt to new business requirements and the seamless integration of convergent mediation for billing processes represents a remarkable milestone in their digital evolution.

This transformative story demonstrates how customers in the public sector and other industries can benefit from the wide ecosystem of industry partner solutions that complement and extend SAP’s robust portfolio of solutions.

For further information, feel free to explore SAP Convergent Mediation by Digital Route and other public sector-specific solutions from our partners.

entry.title: Re: SF Comp Publishing Code - additional conditions in th...
entry.content:
Hi @AlinaTalvescu 

Check out the blog : https://blogs.sap.com/2020/06/11/manage-exclusions-when-publishing-back-to-employee-central/ and if you don't think that the workaround in it will still NOT work for you then unfortunately you will have no other choice but to add custom columns with formula on the worksheet to do the filtering you want.

A classic example is when you have one country out of 50 that requires a publish back to several pay components instead of one. In that case you need to create as many custom columns as there are pay components in EC to publish back to with custom formula (if paycomponent=ABC,finSalary,0) so you can send the right amount to its matching paycomponent in the xml for publish back.

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Avatax Rates by Address in SAP Business One
entry.content:
Is anyone using the native SAP Business One Avatax Rates by Address integration problem free and able to use it through the DIAPI?

We are getting intermittent 'Cannot connect to the Electronic Document Service' errors when using it through the SAP Client and no consistent failures when adding or updating records through the DIAPI.

It falls into the support gap between SAP and Avalara and our SAP Dev Partner has reached the end of his tether trying to diagnose/isolate exactly where/why it fails.

As an aside - it is unable to cope with the full 9 digit USA zip code - it only supports the first 5 characters, but is not smart enough to trim along zip code.

If anyone has this problem or has crossed this bridge, please let get in touch?

grant@delecuona.com

entry.title: Re: Condition Contract for Sales - Manual Delta Accruals
entry.content:
Here’s a revised version of your response:

Hi @Miche1984 ,

Thank you for your detailed question.

To clarify, in standard SAP behavior, when posting a manual delta accrual, there is no need to adjust the Condition Type Group for Accruals, as the system automatically updates the condition type to REA1 in the WCOCOF table. Therefore, including RENT in that group is unnecessary.

Regarding the behavior you’re encountering, it is important to note that standard configuration will not likely meet the expectations you described. The automatic settlement process inherently focuses on business volume transactions/documents. Since your manual delta accrual is an adjustment not tied to any business volume, subsequent automatic settlements (whether delta accrual, partial, or final) will not recognize it as part of the business volume. Instead, it will reverse the manual adjustment and recalculate the correct amount based on the business volume and conditions defined in the contract.

To ensure that the next automatic settlement considers additional adjustments, you could:

  1. Create a Business Volume Document: Generate a document that represents the required adjustment and can be treated as business volume for the contract. This approach allows the automatic settlement to calculate the adjusted amount correctly.

  2. Use a Rebate Adjustment Condition Type (e.g., REJ1): Add a rebate adjustment condition directly in the contract to reflect the adjustment. However, keep in mind that maintaining this condition can be resource-intensive if frequent adjustments are required (e.g., weekly or monthly for the same contract).

I hope this helps clarify the system's standard behavior and provides a path forward. Let me know if further assistance is needed!

Best regards,
Gauthier

entry.title: Event-Driven Multi-Tenant Architecture on SAP BTP with CAP and SAP Event Mesh
entry.content:
Introduction

One of the key fundamentals of cloud native applications is that they are not monolithic, which means they are composed by a series of microservices – each one performing a very specific task – and those microservices are loosely-coupled - meaning they essentially do not depend on each other to achieve their outcomes.

A common technique to achieve loosely-coupling is to make microservices communicate with each other through what’s called event messaging: an event that is triggered by one service – e.g. creation or updating of a service entity – generates a message that is “published” (or posted if you will) to an “event message broker” which, then, stores it in a “message queue” to be consumed by another services. Such message contains relevant information about the event – e.g. the unique identifier of the entity for which the event was triggered.

An example of open-source message broker is Apache Kafka which handles messages that follow the CloudEvents specification.

SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) offers four different message broker services: SAP Event Mesh, SAP Event Mesh on Integration Suite, SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh and SAP Cloud Application Event Hub. These services also implement the CloudEvents specification. The choice for one of them depends mostly on the characteristics of each use case where event messaging is required.

The Business Problem

Although those services are quite robust, reliable, and resilient in compliance with cutting-edge cloud application standards, they do not offer the capability of being consumed by multi-tenant applications, which means they are not “tenant-aware” like the destination and the authorization and trust management (XSUAA) services, among others.

For a better understanding of the issue, let’s picture the following business scenario: imagine you have two different customers running on different S/4HANA Cloud instances – let’s call them S4-1 and S4-2 - which are publishing event messages (called topics) to one of the message broker services instantiated in a subaccount on SAP BTP.

Now, let’s suppose that same subaccount serves as provider of a multi-tenant application.

Then, you create two different subscriptions of such multi-tenant application – each one in its corresponding consumer subaccount - and you want the first subscription to receive messages from S4-1 and the second from S4-2 – like you do when a subscription consumes APIs from S4-1 and another from S4-2 via the destination service.

As the message brokers from BTP are not tenant-aware - like the destination service -, that scenario is not possible by default.

The Solution

The fact that you have services which are not tenant-aware doesn’t mean that, with some creativity, you cannot overcome such obstacle. You can solve such kind of situation by simply putting a service in-between the non-tenant-aware service and the multi-tenant application and make such service tenant-aware.

A tenant-aware service requires some kind of “tenant registration” to control which tenant should be served on each request. That’s basically the “backbone” of a tenant-aware service, which is then enriched with other multi-tenant capabilities.

In our case we will develop an “event message dispatcher” service which will act as a kind of “hub” that consumes the messages published by “n” different S/4HANA Cloud tenants to a message queue of a client and then, distribute (dispatch) them to their corresponding subscriber (consumer) tenant.

Well, you might ask: “how do I tie an S/4HANA Cloud tenant to a subscriber tenant?”. Simple: if you inspect the CloudEvents specification, you’ll notice that every message must contain the so-called “context attributes”, one of them being the “source” of the event. That attribute must be unique for each message context, meaning that each S/4HANA Cloud tenant has a unique id for messages published when an event is triggered by an action performed on a BO - such Business Partner (e.g. BP created, or BP changed). For example: when a Business Partner is created on S4-1 the event “source” will be different than the “source” of a Business Partner created on S4-2, but always the same for the Business Partner BO (e.g. S4-1 issues source “/default/sap.s4.beh/740613139” whenever a BP is created or changed, whereas S4-2 issues source “/default/sap.s4.beh/740740843” for those same events).

Therefore, we use the source attribute of the message context to tie each S/4HANA Cloud tenant to a corresponding subscriber tenant in a BTP subaccount.

As previously mentioned, a tenant-aware service must provide some kind of “tenant registration” mechanism to control which tenant should be served on each request. In this specific case, the “request” is replaced by the message consumption from the message queue as it’s not coming from the subscriber, but, instead, being “pushed” to it. So, upon creation of a new subscription of the “multi-tenant event message consumer” application, a “registration” endpoint from the message dispatcher is called passing the relevant tenant information to subsequently tie it to a message source. The dispatcher, then, stores that information in its database and uses it to control the distribution of messages to their corresponding tenants according to the message source.

Solution Architecture

Let’s do a deep dive into the solution architecture for a better understanding of the big picture:

  1. An SAP Event Mesh service instance is created and configured in the provider subaccount. This represents the “message client” in which a message queue is created and “subscribed” to message topics from S/4HANA Cloud. Each S/4HANA Cloud tenant is configured to publish topics to the same message client using its “service key” to setup a communication arrangement.
  2. When the messages are received by the message client, they are stored in the subscribed message queue and then, consumed by the event message dispatcher service (which is a CAP Node.js service).
  3. A consumer subaccount creates a subscription of the event message consumer app (which is also a CAP Node.js service with a Fiori Elements UI).
  4. The SaaS Provisioning Service bound to the event message consumer app handles the subscription creation, whilst the Service Manager takes care of the tenant data isolation in SAP HANA Cloud through a specific database schema for each tenant.
  5. The SaaS Provisioning Service, then, invokes the “on subscription” callback endpoint from the event message consumer app which will, in turn, call the tenant registration endpoint from the event message dispatcher service via destination passing the relevant tenant information, which is stored in the dispatcher database (in this architecture, also SAP HANA Cloud). This communication is secured by the Authentication and Trust Management (XSUAA) service.
  6. When the event message dispatcher service consumes a message from the queue it takes the message source and uses it to search the registration database to find the corresponding tenant subscription. When the tenant is registered in the database the source is set to “new-tenant”, which means that this mechanism works on a “first-come-first-served” mode, hence if the dispatcher does not find the source in the database, it takes the first “new-tenant” source registered and ties the incoming source to it. Using the tenant information tied to the message source, the dispatcher calls a “consume endpoint” from the event message consumer app subscription, also via destination, passing the message data. This communication is also secured by the XSUAA service.
  7. When a user of the app accesses a subscription the Application Router is invoked using the subscription URL.
  8. The Application Router authenticates the user via XSUAA service. Authentication is performed by the configured Identity Provider of the subaccount with a SAML (or OpenID) response to XSUAA which, in turn, issues a JWT that’s forwarded to the event message consumer app. The app, after having processed the messages received from the dispatcher, presents the outcomes to the user via UI.

The Solution Prototype

We have developed a prototype implementing the reference architecture above to demonstrate how it works in practice and serve as a starting point for your own even-driven multi-tenant application. The prototype uses the “create” and “change” event message topics from the Business Partner BO, but, of course, the concept can be applied to other BOs as well.

The source code of the prototype is published to this Git Repo. There you’ll find detailed instructions on how to install and deploy it to your own BTP landscape.

The purpose of this blog post is not to go into the details of the code implementation, so, please, feel free to explore the source code as you wish. It’s fully commented and kind of self-explanatory.

In this post we will focus only on the key aspects of the destinations used in the implementation, because it’s there where the real magic lies.

Solution Destinations

The first one is the Event-Dispatcher destination, which is used by the event message consumer app to perform tenant registration:

  1. After you have deployed the event message dispatcher service to Cloud Foundry, you get the service URL from the deployed application and put it here.
  2. Select the XSUAA service binding of the application, view the sensitive data (service key), get the client id and client secret, and put them here.
  3. From the same service key get the authentication URL, append “/oauth/token” to it and put it here.

There is no mystery in this one: as the service is secured by XSUAA we just use the client credentials and the token service URL to authenticate to the registration endpoint and post the tenant data to it. So, as you may have seen in the Git Repo instructions you must first deploy the event message dispatcher service and then create the above destination.

The trick is in the second destination: Event-Consumer used by the event message dispatcher service to dispatch the message to the event message consumer app subscription.

  1. After you have deployed the event message consumer app to Cloud Foundry, you get the service URL from the deployed application and put it here. However, this one will be dynamically replaced by the event message dispatcher service with the tenant URL that’s been provided upon tenant registration.
  2. Select the XSUAA service binding of the application, view the sensitive data (service key), get the client id and client secret, and put them here.
  3. From the same service key get the authentication URL, append “/oauth/token” to it and put it here, but this time, replace the “sub domain” part of it with the word “subDomain”. And here is where the trick lies: this word is also dynamically replaced by the tenant (subaccount) sub domain that’s provided upon tenant registration.

You might be wondering: “why is that? with URL and token service URL being dynamically replaced this destination serves exclusively to securely store the client credentials?”.

Well, it’s basically that… however, it’s necessary because the “tenant id” for subscription authentication and data isolation is encoded in the JWT issued by XSUAA. Therefore, if you keep the provider app URL in the URL and the provider sub domain in the token service URL you’ll be authenticating in the provider and not in the consumer, thus generating a JWT with the provider tenant id instead of the consumer. This would prevent the event message dispatcher to properly dispatch the message to the corresponding tenant and the event consumer app to properly store the information in the corresponding database schema.

Conclusion

After reading through this blog post (and possibly deploying and inspecting the prototype source code) you may have learnt what’s an event-driven cloud solution and how to implement an event-driven multi-tenant architecture.

We hope you have enjoyed the ride and can take advantage of these learnings in a near future.

Thanks for reading!

entry.title: Expert Feedback: Options for changing a credit card liability type within Concur
entry.content:
Hi all, as a Technical Consultant at Concur I setup a lot of credit card feeds for our customers.  One common question that I get is related to updating the liability type on an existing credit card feed.   Specifically, our customers want to know whether they need to purchase a new feed with Concur or whether we can make changes to the existing one they already have.  This can be a complex issue and there are several things to consider.   In this blog post, I will attempt to address this question using the most common scenarios that we see.   

Ok…before we get too deep into the topic, I want to start by defining the different liability types that are available.  Hopefully these will be familiar to you.   I have seen occasions where a customer may use a slightly different name for a liability type, but in the end they always align to one of these.

  • IBIP (Individual Bill Individual Pay) – This type of credit card is very much like an employee’s personal card.  These cards are the employee’s personal liability, and they are responsible for making payments to them whenever a bill comes due.   When this type of card is used in a business environment, it is the employee’s responsibility to expense any business transactions that they made so they can be reimbursed the funds they need to pay the card for those expenses.   Within Concur, a credit card that is setup with an IBIP liability type will trigger a cash reimbursement payable to the employee.
  • IBCP (Individual Bill Company Pay) -  This type of credit card has a shared liability with the company.   The employee will receive the statements for the credit card, but the expectation is that the company will be making payments on the employee’s behalf based on submitted expense reports.   If the employee does not submit expense reports for this type of card, the account will go delinquent, and both the employee and company can be held liable.  
  • CBCP (Company Bill Company Pay) – This type of card is the liability of the company.   The company is responsible for making payments for this type of card monthly.   The company normally makes their payment from a billing statement that is produced by the credit card vendor.   The company will make this payment regardless of whether the employee has submitted expense reports detailing the types of expenses that were incurred.  Most of the time, a company will complete a reconciliation between the amounts paid to the credit card vendor and the amounts submitted on expense reports to ensure that un-expensed transactions do not grow over time. 

Now that we are aligned on the different liability types available, I’ll get back to the original question about changing the liability type of an existing feed.  Here are the four most common scenarios that we see when making a liability type change.  These scenarios apply to all of the liability types mentioned above and the process does not differ based on liability type.

  • The credit card vendor plans to issue new cards to card holders using a new feed.
  • The credit card vendor plans to issue new cards to card holders using the existing feed.
  • The credit card vendor plans to move existing cards into a new feed with Concur.
  • The credit card vendor plans to use the existing cards in the existing feed. 

I have placed these options in order from most desirable to least desirable in terms of following our best practices.   Read on below as I go through each one.

Option 1: The credit card vendor issues new cards to card holders using a new feed.

From a Concur perspective, this is really the best-case scenario.   The issuing of new card accounts opens the door for the assignment of a new payment type in Concur.   The payment type controls the liability type used on the card and hence determines Concur’s processing method for transactions on that given card.

This is the cleanest approach because it allows for a clean cutover between the old liability type and the new one.   All activity under the old liability type would continue to flow through Concur as it normally would and the new transactions with the new liability type will come in on the new feed just like those from a completely different credit card vendor.  Transactions for both the old and new liability type can exist within Concur at the same time without any issue and there is no cleanup or blackout period required.  

This approach is also the cleanest from a reporting standpoint.   Since the payment type name is created and a new feed was added to Concur, you have two additional data points that can be used to separate activity being processed under the old liability type vs the new one.  Yes, there is a relatively small fee associated with the setup of the new feed, but as you read through these options you will see that in most cases this fee is offset by a reduction in overall effort required for the transition.

Option 2:  The credit card vendor issues new cards to card holders using the existing feed.

Like with option 1, the assignment of new card accounts provides an opportunity for Concur to assign a new payment type for the new cards.   However, since the existing feed is going to be re-used a short blackout period is needed to ensure that all transactions made under the old liability type are processed through to Concur before the payment type can be updated on the credit card feed. 

Concur will need to stop the credit card feed and receive confirmation from the bank regarding the name of the first file that contains transactions with the new liability type.   This is critical because the payment type that is setup with the import job needs to be updated, but it can not be updated until the new file is received.   From a processing standpoint, any transaction that refers to the old payment type will continue to carry the old liability type and the new cards received in the first new file will contain the new payment type and its associated new liability type.  

Transactions can be processed within the same expense report because the assignment of a new payment type ensures that Concur processes the new transactions differently from the old ones.   From a reporting standpoint, the new payment type name can be used to distinguish the old liability type from the new one.      

Option 3:  The credit card vendor moves existing cards into a new feed with Concur.

This is really only a viable option if the number of active cards and outstanding credit card transactions in the system is relatively low.   Let me explain. 

Once a transaction has been imported into Concur and added to an expense report that transaction will retain its assigned liability type even if the associated payment type is changed after the fact.   As a result, all of the transactions currently loaded into the system must be pushed through to posting before any new transactions that should carry the new liability type are imported.      

This requirement usually results in a blackout period where the feed is stopped and all new files that should be processed with the new liability type are held back from being imported while the customer works on the cleanup of the old activity.   Care should be taken to ensure that all transactions are cleaned up in the site and the assistance of someone from Concur may be needed to ensure that all transactions are accounted for. 

Once the cleanup is complete, the customer has two options. 

  1. Re-use the existing payment type and just update the liability type.
  2. Create a new payment type with the new liability type to use with the new feed.

Re-using the existing payment type is possible, but is not recommended because it makes it more difficult to identify transactions that were processed with the old liability type vs the new one.  Since a new feed is also being setup under this option, it is possible to incorporate the old or new feed into any reporting that is being done, but the payment type itself is a much more recognizable and widely understood data point than the credit card import feed.

Creating a new payment type makes the reporting piece easier as discussed above but it does come with a price.  Once a card is assigned to a payment type, the only way that it can be changed is through the UI in Concur.   It cannot be updated through an interface.   This means that even if the payment type was updated on the credit card feed, it will not update the existing accounts that have already been assigned a payment type.  This associated manual update is why we would only consider this type of conversion for customers that have less than 1500 credit cards in use.

Option 4: The credit card vendor uses the existing cards in the existing feed. 

This is the least preferred option and the most difficult to carry out.   It is very similar to option three and carries all of the restrictions and downsides mentioned in option three.  Specifically, it should only be considered if the number of cards and outstanding transactions is relatively low.  A blackout and cleanup period will be required and you will lose all ability to accurately report on transactions being processed under the old liability type vs the new liability type unless you go through with making the manual updates associated with setting up a new payment type mentioned above.    

Wrap up:

Ok…so that is it.   I hope you found this blog post useful.   There are some small variations possible on each of the scenarios mentioned above, but I have tried stick with best practices and feel confident that these cover the primary scenarios you will face.  Please note that if you have an open project with Concur, your assigned Project Manager and Technical Consultant can walk you through each step in the process.   We know that there is already a lot of change required on your side when it comes to updating the credit card liability type and we want to help make the transition as easy as possible for you.

entry.title: Re: open same reports with different parameter values
entry.content:
Hi Shlomi,

There's a few problems with the way you allow users to run those reports...

First issue is Cr for Enterprise is going away, have a look at page 17 on this link:

https://d.dam.sap.com/a/ovSp6DP/BI%20Maintenance%20Strategy%202024.pdf?rc=67&inline=true

BI 2025 removed components
We will not deliver the following components with SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025
Components Go to solution
Universe Design Tool (UDT) – .UNV universe Conversion to UNX (IDT) is strongly recommended (Blog)
Data Federator (DF) : Multi-source universes Several SAP Solutions available (Blog)
SAP BusinessObjects Analysis, edition for OLAP (AOLAP) Several SAP Solutions available (Blog)
Live Office (LO) WebI OData for Excel, Analysis Office (Blog)
Crystal Reports for Enterprise (CR4E) CR2020, WebIntelligence (Blog)

I highly recommend opening your CR4E reports in the desktop version of Crystal Reports and begin converting them, note: you can open CR4E reports in Crystal Reports 2020 Designer, SDK's won't open them though so manually is your only option.

Back to your issue, did it ever work the way you/they wanted it to?

How are they setting the new parameter value?

How are they open multiple copies of the report? Example View Report for the basic report or a scheduled instance etc.?

Are you using any SDK's or simply opening the reports by selecting and clicking on Preview?

Need more details of the work flow...

By default opening Reports via the URL has limitations, when they open the same report using multiple tab's it's the same instance so they won't use multiple parameters unless you can prompt per use. By viewing the same instance it's always going to pass the same values, in your case it doesn't seem to be accepting your parameter value in the URL. Look there for debugging the issue first.

Next steps depends on the workflow they are doing...

Don

I tried to Configure this error by following this process (Transaction SM30, enter table TPAER, click Customizing and it will take you to the IMG) but I don’t know what would be the next step. In IMG screen can any please help me solve this error with proper step's by step process.

MOHSIN

entry.title: Re: Enhancements in EBS - BADI FIEB_CHANGE_BS_DATA
entry.content:
Hi @JazzF , 
Hm... that sounds strange. Right now I do now have opportunity to test it specifically, but I would suggest to do a bit of debugging and check what happens with the adjusted value and why it is overwritten. Normally, that would be the first step to do. 

On the other hand, I would not suggest deleting anything from the bank statement. This way, you're deleting potentially useful information, which might have some relevancy. If you're requirements is just to adjust the line item text, you can implement your own logic either in FEB00001 or in BADI FIEB_CHANGE_BS_DATA. To do that, you will have to adjust the value in the parameter FEBEP-SGTXT. You have the access to FEBRE-table in the interfaces of both enhancement points, you can read the content and adjust the values flexibly for the line item text where it is needed. 

I had similar requirement on one of the projects. The requirement was a bit simplified. I had to delete from line item text some fixed values and to capitalize all letters. So that's what I did via FEBEP-parameter. 
I hope it helps you with your inquiry !

Regards,

Bohdan

entry.title: Amount field length extension in S4HC
entry.content:
Dear Community, 

Is it possible to extend the length of amount fields?

There a blogs about this feature in S4H, but I do not see the equivalent for S4H Cloud.

entry.title: Re: Condition Contract for Sales - Manual Delta Accruals
entry.content:
Dear Gauthier,

First of all, I would like to sincerely thank you for the assistance you’ve provided. We are facing significant challenges with this new feature.

Based on you feedback, our scenario appears to be a use case not covered by the standard functionality, despite it being a common request among users across various industries (posting accruals without quantities that must be reversed during the final settlement run).

I have some additional questions and comments:

1) Regarding the first method "Create a Business Volume Document". Could you clarify what type of document we can be created? Are you suggesting a sort of "fake" billing document that artificially increases the business volume through reported quantities?

1) For the secondo method, "Use a Rebate Adjustment Condition Type (e.g., REJ1)" I guess it can be applied in the data migration phase: we can create a condition contract that includes an REJ1 condition "to offset" the accruals that we'll post through WB2R_DOC_ENTRY to migrate accruals recorded in SAP ECC).

Thank you again for your support, and I look forward to your guidance on this matter.

Best regards,
Michele

entry.title: Display percentage for a calculated measure in Datasphere
entry.content:
Solution:

Assigning percentage to the calculated measure can be done at Datasphere or in the SAC story.

In Datasphere Fact View

The calculated measure can be displayed as percentage by following the steps given below in Datasphere

In the datasphere fact model create a calculated measure and calculate the percentage.

Create a calculated column  and assign '%' as constant value

 

Changes the semantics for the calculated column . Choose Unit of Measure  as semantic type

For the percentage calculated Column , choose the semantic type as 'Quantity with Unit' and  assign  the calculated column created in step 3.

 

Create analytical model using the fact table. 

Create a story in SAC using the datasphere analytical model. Use the calculated column in chart or table.

calculated measure displays the percentage in the widgets.

In SAC story

Similarly in SAC , if there is a use case to display % for a calculated measure, follow the steps given below

  1. Create a calculated measure in SAC story on the Datasphere analytic model.
  2. Call the calculated key figure that is created in Datasphere into the calculated measure created in SAC.
  3. Go to the formatting of the SAC calculated measure that is created and select the options as shown below.
  • Uncheck the option “Use unit of underlying measure’ and chose “Percentage” as Scale.
  • Provide ‘0’ in decimal places.(optional or choose as per the requirement)

  • Remove the actual key figure from the story .
  • Select the calculated measure on the story and change the styling option as shown below

  • Calculated Measure will display as % in the story.

It is preferred to format the measure at the source if it has to be used at different stories instead of recreating again.

entry.title: Date picker unresponsive in SAP Mobile Start (but works well in desktop browser)
entry.content:
Dear experts,

In our app there is an oData function import with an import parameter of type date. When the action is initiated, an input field is rendered with datepicker value help, and it opens the usual calendar and clockface dialog:

This works perfectly in the desktop browsers but on mobile devices via SAP Start there is a strange malfunction:

  • the first time the action popup is opened, date picker works normally, i.e. as in screenshots above
  • but if the user performs following sequence:
    1. picks a date
    2. then cancels the action
    3. then reopens the action again,

        the date picker dialog no longer appears. The datepicker icon is seemingly activated when touched (it briefly flickers in colour when you press), but it is unresponsive.

  • if the action is cancelled again and the page is refreshed, everything is again back to normal.

This happens on both Android and iOS.

Please suggest what could be the problem, or how to analyse further. I am currently trying to remote-debug it on Android and I will post any interesting findings.

Update: in remote debugging this comes up:

adding element with duplicate id 'ActionUtil-BLAHBLAHDIBLAH_MY_SERVICE_Entities-<My_Action_Name>-<My_Parameter_Name>-input-PC' -

Thank you, 

BR Petra

entry.title: Innovation Day 2024 Recap and Happy Holidays
entry.content:
It may be a little late to go back to an event that we already did at the beginning of November. But that does not mean it is not totally worth it. Approaching the end of the year it is always worthwhile to take a step back and look at everything that has happened. But this time I want to look back even a little bit further. SAP Women in Tech has already existed for more than 6 years now. Throughout this time, we have been able to build an incredible network, have seen countless amazing speakers and put together our fair share of well-received events.

This November we finally put everything together with a full day event including a total of 15 sessions and 22 speakers: Our SAP Women in Tech Innovation Day 2024. If I had to describe it with one sentence I would probably say: “The best of both worlds”. Meaning we combined the two strongest topics that we have been working on for the past years. Technology and helping to upskill female speakers and putting them on stages. Needless to say, that with this powerful combination we had a huge turnout and countless attendees that day. It was overall the biggest on-site event we have ever done for SAP Women in Tech and the feedback was incredibly positive.

Looking now towards 2025 one thing is for sure. There is going to be another Innovation Day and we will continue to upskill our female speakers at SAP so that eventually, we will see enough of them on stages across all areas.

All that is left to say is Happy Holidays to everyone reading this and we will see you next year!

entry.title: How is maximum length at /SAPAPO/MAT1 really working?
entry.content:
Hello SAP experts,

I want to ask for your support with a question about the maximum length found in the material master data on EWM S/4HANA (/SAPAPO/MAT1, or MM03).

According to the documentation, this field specifies the maximum length of the products that can be packed inside the packaging material, and it differs from the length of a unit of the packaging material.

For testing purposes, I have maintained 4 CM as maximum length in the packaging material CTN_02 and I have packed in /SCWM/PACK a product with length 706 MM. I was expecting an error message, or at least a warning, but the system allowed me to go ahead with no restriction at all.

Once packed, the handling unit now had 24 CM of length, which is the length in the master data for the packaging material. But the product, which has 70.6 CM as length, was packed successfully into a packaging material with 4 CM as max. length.

So how does this maximum length actually works? Is is just an statistical value, or is it really restricting any products with longer lengths to be packed? If this should show an error/warning message, can you share a case where it actually works?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Error when contingent worker is hired as permanent em...
entry.content:
Hi, could it be that when you were seeing the old record's username updated to username_INACT in the past (e.g. if it was in a previous company), that this was something that someone did manually, or you had some kind of custom configuration to do this? 

In our setup, when we have colleagues who go from contingent worker to regular employee, and we want to use the same account details, we need to go into Manage Login Accounts and scramble the username on the contingent worker record (we do employeeID@company.com). That way, AD can send back the username on the new record without it being blocked due to being a duplicate.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, December 19th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/12/19/sap-developer-news-december-19th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

SAP Developer News End of Year Schedule

New in SAP SDKs for AI

  • SAP Cloud SDK for AI (JavaScript): https://github.com/SAP/ai-sdk-js/releases/
  • SAP Cloud SDK for AI (Java): https://github.com/SAP/ai-sdk-java/releases
  • Generative AI Hub SDK (Python): https://help.sap.com/doc/generative-ai-hub-sdk/CLOUD
  • Understand the World of AI with SAP Learning: https://community.sap.com/t5/artificial-intelligence-and-machine-learning-blogs/understand-the-world-of-ai-with-sap-learning/ba-p/13968267

New series: The Art and Science of CAP with Daniel Hutzel

  • Episodes and show notes https://qmacro.org/blog/posts/2024/12/06/the-art-and-science-of-cap/
  • Live stream series playlist https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQAe45xlhIfhTYB9G0mdRVjI

Extending SAPUI5 Apps

  • Blog post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/extending-sapui5-apps-adaptation-projects-vs-extension-projects/ba-p/13961264

SAP Data & Analytics Advisory Methodology

  • SAP Data & Analytics Advisory Methodology Overview | SAP Help Portal: https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-btp-guidance-framework/sap-data-methodology/sap-data-analytics-advisory-methodology-overview
  • Highlights of SAP Datasphere in 2024: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/activity-7275068742051450881-iHTy
  • Watch out for our Q4 release for SAP HANA Cloud: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/thomashammersap_saphanacloud-sapbtp-saphana-activity-7272528046564282368-t2GE

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 Dev News Schedule
1:01 New in SAP SDKs for AI
2:27 New series: The Art and Science of CAP with Daniel Hutzel
3:52 Extending SAPUI5 Apps
4:49 SAP Data & Analytics Advisory Methodology

Transcription

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for December 19th, 2024.

[Tom] I just want to let everybody know about the upcoming plans for the developer news as we take our annual end of year break. This is the last regular news episode that we'll have for 2024. And like the previous years, we have special year and review episodes coming up for the last week of December and the first week of December. January. So you hear from each of the team members about what they reflect upon 2024, their major accomplishments, things that they've enjoyed, all those sort of things. And be on the lookout for our special bloopers. We'll throw some fun, exciting bloopers in at the end of each of those episodes and probably release that separately as well. So we look forward to seeing you back here for new episodes the second week of January 2025.

[Witalij] SAP Cloud SDK for AI is our official software development kit for SAP AI core, SAP Generative AI hub and orchestration service. The JavaScript TypeScript version was introduced two months ago, and last week we released version 1.4. This new version odds support for the orchestration services grounding capabilities. Grounding allows you to integrate external, contextually, relevant, domain-specific or real-time data into your AI processes. This data supplements the natural language processing capabilities of pre-trained models, which are typically trained only on general data sets. Additionally, the Java version of the SDK has reached a significant milestone with the release of version 1.0, bringing numerous new features and enhancements. You can find the complete documentation and sample projects in the Git repository. For Python users, the Generative AI Hub SDK has recently released version 4.1.1, which is a new introduces support for prompt registry templates in orchestration. I hear feeling overwhelmed by all these terminology. No worries. If you are new to AI are wondering how SAP is embedding AI into our portfolio, check out the  recent post by my teammate Nora. She guides you through relevant courses and activities available on SAP learning. Links are provided in the description.

[DJ] If you've ever wanted to gain a deeper of CAP. Learn more about its design and the fundamental core computing building blocks upon which it's based. Then we've got a series for you. On the Hands on SAP Dev show, which we've been running since early 2019, we've got a new mini -series with none other than the creator of CAP himself, Daniel Hutzel. The series is called the Art and Science of CAP. and in each episode, Daniel takes us on journeys deep and wide, bringing enlightenment and wonder in equal measure. Now, the series was kicked off during Devtoberfest 2024. It's still running. There's an episode or two to go. And alongside the episodes, the live stream episodes, there are also show notes that you can dive into afterwards. Or you can just watch the replays of the live stream episodes. and dive into the show notes as well. There's a ton of stuff to explore and learn about. So, this holiday season, treat yourself to some amazing insights and check out the episodes that we have on offer along with your company notes. And we'll see you online.

[Michelle] Hey y'all. If you're a developer working with SAPUI5, you want to check out this blog post about extension projects versus adaption projects. It's all about how SAP is moving towards a more advanced way of customizing apps with adapt projects. The post explains how Adaption Projects created an SAP Business Application Studio lets you adapt apps without modifying the standard code. You get powerful new features, improved upgrade stability, and the flexibility to work with on any SAP app, not just legacy ones. It also compares the older extension projects to adaption projects showing why adaption projects are the way forward and to give you a better idea of the differences between the two. If you're working with existing extension projects, don't worry, you can still use them, but SAP recommends migrating to an adaption project for bigger or new requirements. To learn more about the blog post, it's linked below and as always, happy coding.

[Witalij] Just in time for the year end, we are happy to share that the SAP Data and Analytics advisory methodology is now officially available as part of the SAP Help Documentation. The methodology provides a comprehensive framework for enterprise and data architects to evaluate data strategies, develop solution architectures and foster a data-driven culture through a structured four-phase approach. This process is supported by a data domain reference model to establish a common understanding of SAP data, a data and analytics capability model to enhance the architectural definition process, and data-driven use-case patterns and reference architecture. to accelerate solution architecture development. Use the holiday season as an opportunity to get inspired and set the groundwork for successful data and analytics projects in the coming year. And while beyond the topic, I'd like to highlight two product-specific resources from the respective managers. The SAP DataSphere 2024 highlights presented by KP Sauer and the teaser for the Q4 release of SAP HANA cloud shared by Thomas Hammer. The links are in the description, as always.

entry.title: Transform Your Business: Use SAP's Free Offering for Process Optimization & Innovation Discovery
entry.content:
So, you have been operating your SAP ERP system for quite some time, and want to prepare for the transformation to SAP S/4HANA? Are you seeking ways to streamline your SAP S/4HANA processes, introduce more innovations like AI to your business users, and fully maximize its value?

We have good news for you: SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition is the perfect starting point for your process improvement and innovation journey.

In a nutshell

The SAP Signavio Process Insights, Discovery Edition is a a one-time free of charge analysis, that delivers personalized insights into the performance of your current business processes, using data from your SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA system. This analysis is crucial for maintaining a competitive edge in today’s dynamic markets, as it identifies opportunities to enhance operational efficiency. By offering detailed insights and tailored recommendations, segmented by line of business and end-to-end processes, it empowers organizations to achieve their efficiency goals effectively.

SPIDE Start Page

SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition is based on the usage and process performance data from your productive ERP or SAP S/4HANA system, and helps you answer the following key questions:

    • Of all the innovations from SAP – SAP Fiori, Business AI, SAP Process Automation, cloud solutions and more – which ones are the most relevant for your business? If you are on SAP ERP: Which SAP S/4HANA capabilities are most relevant for your transformation journey?
    • What is the potential to improve the performance of your business processes? How do you compare to your peers and how to do it?

SPIDE Recommendations Summary

If you want to have a short overview (6 Mins). I recommend watching this introduction video:

How to Order 

In order to request SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition your SAP basis team needs to do the following:

    1. Check the prerequisites, especially ST-A/PI version
    2. Implement the latest version of SAP Notes 2745851 and 2758146 in productive SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA system
    3. Run data extraction report and download ZIP file
    4. Request your analysis here: https://url.sap/discovery-edition.
    5. After five business days, you receive access to your discovery edition including the Executive Summary.

Start your Review

In the Get-Started Guide, you will find an overview on how to review your analysis. In addition, we recommend reading the blog series which will comprehensively explain all important aspects with the help of visuals of SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition analysis.

Further Information

Use the Reference Guide for Business Content in SAP Signavio Process discovery edition to get detailed information about all available process performance indicators. You can access the guide from directly within SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition application.

Find more enablement material in this presentation. Check our frequently asked questions document to get a quick answer to query.

To broaden your knowledge, we recommend reading these blogs:

Executive summary
Part I: Introductory blog designed for business decision makers to get a comprehensive understanding of the discovery edition: Receive and present a clear picture of your proces... - SAP Community

Part II: New topics within the Executive summary (AI recommendations, HR recommendations): NEW PDF Executive Summary now delivered by default... - SAP Community

Blog series of SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition
Consisting of a total of eleven blogs, the series explains all important aspects of SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition analysis, with the help of visuals: Introducing Blog Series of SAP Signavio Process In... - SAP Community

Start your transformation journey to SAP S/4HANA:
Part I: This blog is relevant for you want to prepare the transformation of your SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA system: Move to SAP S/4HANA with SAP Signavio Process Insi... - SAP Community

Part II: This blog is relevant for you when you have already an existing SAP S/4HANA system and aim for updating it to the latest version: Innovations for live SAP S/4HANA systems with SAP ... - SAP Community

Do you have further questions? Just reach out to us directly: Signavio-Discovery@sap.com

entry.title: Mass change in maintain business roles
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

Is there any option to mass change in maintain business roles in SAP Public Cloud?

My requirement is to maintain restrictions, such as plant, company code, and movement types, in bulk for multiple users at once. Currently, it takes too much time to update these restrictions individually for each user role.

 

Thanks


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Mass change in maintain business roles
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Yes, it is possible to make mass changes to Business Roles. Please check the procedure below:

  1. Select the business roles you want to change and click Mass Change button. The Mass Change Wizard appears. 
  2. Select the required attributes.
  3. Click Add Restrictions or Remove Restrictions.
  4. Copy the required restrictions using the Copy Restriction Values From and the Copy Restriction Values To value help.
  5. Select additional access categories if needed.
  6. Apply the required attributes to your business roles.
  7. Review and confirm your changes.

 

For more information, check SAP Help Portal | How to Make Mass Changes to Business Roles

 

Kind regards.

entry.title: CIR MD81 configurations
entry.content:

Further on configuration issue:

    sales_cfgs_refinst) . ( BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT  is called after this!)

2. b) Then I tried calling FM CE_C_PROCESSING in addition to 2.a), this throws error as instance is not populated from VBAP-CUOBJ

2. c) Thirdly i tried calling FM V45CU_CONFIGURATION  in addition to point 2.a) . I get "Internal error in communication between configuration and sales doc.GET_CONFIG_MODE" because of VBAP-CUOBJ being blank and this is supposed to be the importing parameter through INSTANCE 

 

Basically below pseudocode is the sequence of FMs i ave tried to use to create CIR with configs and has failed. 

BAPI_CFG_CREATE

BAPI_CFG_READ

CUX1_CHARACTS_READ

SD_SALESDOCUMENT_CREATE

BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT

CE_C_PROCESSING.

Any inputs would be helpful. please let me know if any clarifications are needed

entry.title: Re: A way to extra information out of SEN CLoud to Azure...
entry.content:
Hi Stephen,

There is a reporting API that can be used to extract information from the SEN database but I suspect that this is not the information that you are looking for.  The API allows you to extract details about content consumption and the meta data of content objects but not the actual content itself.  Anyway, have a look at the contents of this book to see if this is helpful.

Given that the contents is for a Chatbot, I suspect that you want the actual content of the objects, e.g. Book Pages.  If that is the case, perhaps you can generate the content in Word format, i.e. a Compound Document, and load it from the Word document into the Azure DB.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Seamless Scaling of SAP HANA on Intel Xeon Processors from Micro to Mega-Databases
entry.content:
Unleashing Business Potential with SAP HANA and Intel Xeon Processors: A Value Proposition for Customers 

In today's fast-paced business environment, the ability to handle, process, and extract insights from vast amounts of data are a competitive necessity. SAP and Intel have collaborated for over a decade, offering unparalleled innovation to organizations aiming to leverage the power of the SAP HANA in-memory database and scalable hardware by Intel.

Our joint efforts to deliver a robust value proposition empower businesses to scale seamlessly from micro to mega-databases. Today, over 90% of global SAP HANA customers run some of the most mission critical solutions in the SAP HANA database, deployed on Intel Xeon processor technology. 

 

High-Performance Processing for Critical Operations 

At the core of this partnership lies the commitment to performance. We optimized SAP HANA’s database for Intel's 4th and 5th Gen Scalable Xeon processors and achieved up to 40% improved performance in transactional queries (OLTP) compared to previous processor generations. This leads to quicker decision-making and the ability to easily process high volumes of concurrent transactions, thus reducing the time to value. Intel's Transactional Synchronization Extensions (TSX), also implemented into the SAP HANA database, further enhances this scalability and offers a significant performance boost for critical HANA database operations.

Being able to run the SAP HANA database with superior performance on a wide range of system configurations, from small servers with a memory footprint of a few GB at a low cost and energy consumption, up to the largest scale-up HANA instances with up to 32 TB of memory footprint deployed, is a unique capability enabled on Intel Xeon-based server platforms. Such configurations are supported in Scale-Up but also in Scale-Out scenarios, enabling the mega database capacities that customers require.

 

Large Servers for Large Databases 

A key feature of the Intel® Xeon® 4th Gen Scalable processor is its extreme scalability. The largest companies in the world are using SAP HANA to run their business-critical database workload. These installations keep growing due to the increase in data size and enhanced capabilities of S/4HANA installations running SAP HANA as a database. 

Intel and SAP meet these performance requirements of SAP HANA customers for their business-critical database applications with ease. For example, SAP internal tests demonstrate up to a 40% higher performance for transactional queries with the 4th and 5th Gen Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor, compared to previous generation.

Intel 4th Gen Xeon is equipped with 4 Ultra Path Interconnect (UPI) lanes between processors to enable additional connectivity and the best customer experience, especially for the largest SAP Customer Landscapes.

Scaling-up to over 8 CPUs with UPI lanes enables the mega database capacities required. As shown in the figure below, the 4th Gen Xeon processors use a dedicated fourth UPI link to improve the connectivity and bandwidth between the Intel Xeon processors. It also enables low latency, which is especially beneficial for SAP HANA’s transactional workloads. The reduced number of hops to a remote processor, that are needed to fetch data from the memory associated with that remote processor, enables improved cross-socket bandwidth, which is essential for low response times of analytical queries. 

With up to 8 processors connected, as shown in the figure above, the SAP hardware partner ecosystem can support systems with up to 16TB of memory and 448 CPU cores. Our joint partners HPE and Eviden even support 4th Gen Xeon-based server platforms with 16 CPUs and up to 32TB, resulting in an impressive 960 CPU cores in a single scale-up system. This is achieved with the support of a node controller that further extends the UPI enabled interconnect to an additional 8 CPUs.

 

Small Cores for Efficiency and Sustainability  

Intel recently launched the all-new Intel® Xeon® 6 with Efficiency Cores (E-Cores, code-name Sierra Forrest), offering a new class of light-weight efficient cores that deliver an optimized performance per watt. SAP and Intel engineers spent the last year validating and certifying SAP HANA on Xeon 6 with E-Cores successfully. A key benefit of Xeon 6 for SAP with E-cores is the impressive performance at a reduced power consumption, helping SAP customers to reduce their carbon footprint. In SAP internal tests, we have observed up to 60% better power efficiency with SAP HANA compared to previous gen processors.  

SAP did not require rewriting or reworking SAP HANA; the already optimized SAP HANA solution runs out of the box on Xeon 6 with E-Cores. As such, no porting of HANA code was needed, since the new Xeon 6 processor remains backward compatible with existing Intel Xeon processors, which are already supported by SAP HANA.

Intel Xeon 6 for SAP with E-cores matches SAP HANA requirements very well and provides a good match for analytical use cases (OLAP). The analytical workload can scale well on the 144 Core Xeon 6 with E-core processor.

  

Intel Processors for Business-Critical Database Workload 

When SAP HANA customers run their business-critical database workload, e.g. a quarter-end closing required for financial reporting, the highest throughput and reliably low response times are critical. Intel processors enable such workloads with various unique features. 

 

Hardware-Assisted Synchronization with Intel TSX 

Intel Transactional Synchronization Extensions (Intel TSX) enable effective synchronization of frequent concurrent accesses to data structures in SAP HANA, since their introduction in 2013. This implementation of hardware transactional memory remains a distinct feature of Intel processors in SAP HANA’s cloud environment. Recent enhancements to Intel TSX allow the temporary disabling of tracking loads from memory in a section of code within a transaction. This simplified further optimizations of the scalability of SAP HANA, and SAP internal tests show a performance upside of up to 1.4x for some workloads. 

 

Revolutionary Speed for Analytical Insights 

 For organizations needing quick insights, SAP HANA uses Intel's advanced Single Instruction Multiple Data (SIMD) technology to speed up SAP HANA’s full-table scan. This enables the search for one or multiple values in a table, achieving a remarkable speed of 560 billion rows per second.

This unprecedented performance redefines analytics, enabling businesses to process vast amounts of data quickly and efficiently. Combined with Intel technologies like Advanced Vector Extension, AVX-512 and Vector Bit-Manipulating Instructions (VBMI), data processing becomes faster, more efficient, and remarkably cost-effective. 

 

Extending the Address Space for More Memory 

In-memory databases like SAP HANA are pushing the limits of memory capacities of servers. Until recently, Intel-based systems with up to 24TB were in use by SAP HANA customers. Although efficient algorithms compress the data in memory, the need for more data continues to drive the demand for even larger systems. The Scalable Xeon architecture, in its 4th and 5th generation, expanded the address space to 57 bits for virtual address space and 52 bits for physical address space. The increase in core count and the expansion of the address space now allow SAP HANA customers to use even larger systems with up to 32TB. This allows SAP HANA to grow further than before and serve the largest Fortune 500 customers. 

 

Future-Proofing with Innovative Memory Solutions   

Looking ahead, the collaboration will redefine memory scalability through emerging technologies, like Compute Express Link (CXL), that has been introduced into the market with the Intel Xeon 6 processor family. Compute express link will revolutionize the data center, like the storage revolution a decade ago. In storage, we evolved from having disks inside the server, to remote disk, to remote virtual disks provided by a central storage solution that mount them to computing nodes. This enabled scalability, flexibility and reduced costs. Similarly, CXL allows accessing memory on a local system or remote system. It enables so-called Memory Pools, that are dynamically linked to computing nodes. Using CXL technology within an SAP HANA system will allow businesses to adopt flexible memory architectures, seamlessly adjusting to diverse workload demands in both on-premises and cloud environments. 

   

Delivering Exceptional Value 

This partnership between SAP and Intel brings more than just technological advancements: it delivers tangible business value. Customers gain access to unparalleled scalability, efficiency, and performance, enabling them to unlock new opportunities, reduce costs, and stay ahead of their competitors. Whether you're a small enterprise or a global conglomerate, SAP HANA powered by Intel Xeon processors equips your business to handle today's challenges and seize tomorrow's opportunities. The question isn't whether you can keep up with the data revolution; it's how far ahead you can get. With SAP and Intel, the answer is straightforward.

 

Notices & Disclaimers

Performance varies by use, configuration and other factors. Learn more on the Performance Index site.
Performance results are based on testing as of dates shown in configurations and may not reflect all publicly available updates.  See backup for configuration details.  No product or component can be absolutely secure.
Your costs and results may vary.
Intel technologies may require enabled hardware, software or service activation.
© Intel Corporation.  Intel, the Intel logo, and other Intel marks are trademarks of Intel Corporation or its subsidiaries.  Other names and brands may be claimed as the property of others.

entry.title: Which tools do you use for CSV files?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I currently have more to do with SuccessFactors HCM than I would like.
Anyway, I have noticed that you have to deal with a lot of CSV files and the separators and date / time formatting is very limited.

So a CSV file from SuccessFactors HCM always uses a comma, if another separator is possible, then this is very rare.
The other is the date / time, here the system is a little more flexible but there are also components that only know MM/DD/YYYY.

Well I work in Europe and the date format MM/DD/YYYYY and the comma separator isn't common for people and MS tools here.
So MS Excel can't be used.

So I wanted to ask around, which tools do you use to edit and view these CSV files?
I explicitly do not want to use MS Excel, as this type of CSV file cannot be handled without technical knowledge

Thanks for your input 😄

Greetings
Andy

entry.title: SAP Dynamic Forms 2411 Released Features
entry.content:
Welcome to the release of SAP Dynamic Forms version 2411.   In this blog, we will introduce you to the new features and provide you with an update on the product roadmap.  

Roadmap 

Below is the latest updated product roadmap, you can also check back later on the SAP Digital Asset portal for updates. 

 New Features  

Let us look at some of the features we are introducing in this release. For a more detailed listing, please refer to the official help documentation of SAP Dynamic Forms on the SAP help portal.    

Key Points 

  • New Industry Form controls in Form Builder to capture Measuring Points and GPS location 
  • Support of Dynamic Forms for S/4HANA Service and Service and Asset Manager on Windows (Android and iOS already supported)  

Industry Form Controls for Measuring Points and GPS coordinates.  

  • Form Controls are elements that allow business users to enter fields in forms 
  • The Measurement Point control allows end users to take readings via on the mobile or web application 
  • GPS control allows end users to enter their location or get a pre-populated location needed for their work. 

 

Pre- and post-processing of GPS location and measuring points requires configuration from the backend 

Support of Dynamic Forms for Service and Asset Manager on Windows 3.0 

  • Form associated with work orders, operations, and suboperations will be downloaded to the device to be completed offline during work execution 

Support of Dynamic Forms for S/4HANA Service  

  • View and complete form instances associated with service orders and items online or offline.  

Support of Dynamic Forms for Maintenance Tasks Lists 

 Limitations 

Dynamic Forms support in Fiori Applications is delayed and is currently being evaluated for a patch release in Q1 2025.   For customers expected to go live with this feature, please reach out to us.    

  • F2071 - Find Maintenance Notification 
  • F2175 - Find Maintenance Order 
  • F5104A - Perform Maintenance Jobs 

Embedded support to provide direct and context aware access to support knowledge base and support channels.   This feature is also delayed and will be enabled in our first patch release in Q1 2025.   

  • Support the ability to automatically create a support incident from within the product. 
  • Usage metrics on SAP4Me now includes user counts

entry.title: Re: AMDP Select with TOP 3 entries based on certain fields
entry.content:
Resolved by myself
Regards

 SELECT TOP 20
        S1.lifnr      AS LIFNR,
        SUM(S1.dmbtr) AS DMBTR,
        SUM(S1.dmbt2) AS DMBT2
   FROM :source AS S1
  GROUP BY lifnr
  ORDER BY DMBT2 DESC
